item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Markets are in a Recovery regime but remain sensitive to inflation and growth signaling. Elevated oil volatility (OVX) and a still-elevated VIX keep tail-risk pricing present; rising bond yields could re-test momentum gains. Position sizing and stop discipline remain important as breadth and sector rotations (e.g., ARKK lagging) could accelerate quickly.
Market summary: - SPY: 694.46, above 200-day MA 665.17 — bullish trend (stable) - QQQ: 628.60, above 100-day MA 609.17 — technology leadership intact - ARKK: three-month change -10.07% — active-growth names lagging recovery - VIX: 18.36 — moderate risk premia (stable) - TNX (10-yr yield): 4.26% — higher yields provide tightening context for equities - OVX: 75.35, regime mid — oil volatility elevated - Mortgage rate: 6.37%, condition: Neutral — mortgage environment unchanged/neutral - Other: breadth and volatility metrics warrant disciplined risk management; raw data available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk Commentary: Recovery momentum visible but mixed risk signals warrant caution. Volatility is moderate and credit/term-premia remain elevated, so position sizing and stop discipline are advised while trend-followers can stay engaged.
Market Summary: - SPY: 686.10 trading above its 200-day MA (664.76) — bullish trend, stable relative to the recovery regime. - QQQ: 617.39 vs 100-day MA 608.92 — near-term strength, confirming tech participation in the rally. - VIX: 19.12 — moderate volatility; stable for now but watch for spikes on macro headlines. - TNX (10yr yield): 4.30% — higher yield backdrop, supportive of financials but a headwind for duration-sensitive assets. - OVX: 80.55 (regime: mid) — oil volatility elevated, could feed through to energy and inflation expectations. - ARKK: three-month change -12.82% — active growth/innovation exposure lagging broader market. - Mortgage rate: 6.37% (condition: Neutral) — explicitly noted; housing financing remains a constraint but condition is neutral. - Macro/positioning: recovery regime with moderate volatility and elevated yields — conditions unchanged/stable for the current snapshot. - Raw data: available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Markets are in a recovery phase but remain sensitive to growth and rate signals. Volatility is elevated versus pre-pandemic norms (VIX ~19) and oil volatility (OVX mid, 78) keeps a risk premium in place. Rising real yields and sticky mortgage costs could cap housing- and consumption-led upside; monitor any jump in VIX or TNX for regime shifts. Raw data available in /data.
Market summary: - Regime: Recovery (🟢) - SPY: 679.46, trading above 200-day MA (664.36) — upward trend but watch for pullbacks - QQQ: 611.07, near its 100-day MA (608.83) — momentum intact but close to the short-term moving average - ARKK: -13.7% over three months — growth/innovation pocket lagging the broader market - VIX: 19.23 — elevated relative to low-volatility environments; risk sentiment cautious but not panic - TNX (10-yr yield): 4.31% — materially higher yields that weigh on duration-sensitive assets and valuations - OVX: 78.01 (regime: mid) — oil volatility elevated, adding uncertainty for energy-linked sectors - Mortgage: 6.37% (condition: Neutral) — mortgage rate and condition stable for now but remain a drag on housing demand - Treasury/yield context: yields elevated and stable at current levels; a sustained rise would increase recession risk and pressure equities - Conditions: broadly stable for the moment, but vulnerable to shocks in volatility, rates, or oil markets

item.title: Market Regime: 🟢 Recovery
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: The market is in a recovery phase but faces mixed signals: equity momentum is modestly positive while volatility and oil-risk measures remain elevated. Rising yields could pressure rate-sensitive sectors and cap gains if they continue upward; however, the S&P and Nasdaq sitting at/above key moving averages supports further risk-on positioning. Maintain position sizing discipline and monitor volatility and yield moves for regime shifts.
Market summary: - Regime: Recovery (🟢) - SPY: 679.91, trading above its 200-day MA (664.0) — trend supportive/stable - QQQ: 610.19, near its 100-day MA (608.81) — momentum holding - ARKK: -14.44% over 3 months — continued weakness in high-beta/innovation names - VIX: 19.49 — moderate volatility; stable for now - TNX (10-yr yield): 4.29% — higher-rate context that could cap valuations if it rises further - OVX: 81.74 (regime: mid) — elevated oil-volatility risk - Mortgage rate: 6.37% (condition: Neutral) — explicitly noted and stable - Action points: monitor VIX and TNX for signs of stress or trend reversal; raw data available in /data.

item.title: Market Regime: 🟡 Mixed Signals
item.description:
This is an automated market signal summary for informational purposes only. It is not financial advice.
Risk commentary: Mixed signals dominate — equity breadth is tepid, volatility is elevated but not panic‑level, and yields remain in a higher regime. That combination keeps upside capped and tail‑risk asymmetric: constructive for selective rotation and income strategies but cautionary for aggressive growth exposure.
Market summary: - SPY: 676.01 trading above its 200‑day MA (663.6) — trend mildly constructive / stable. - QQQ: 606.09 sits just below its 100‑day MA (608.91) — technology under slight pressure. - VIX: 21.04 — elevated vs long‑run lows, signaling higher realized/expected dispersion but not crisis. - TNX (10y yield): 4.29% — yields remain high relative to recent years, keeping discount rates elevated for growth stocks. - ARKK (3‑month): -13.29% — pronounced drawdown in high‑beta/innovative names. - OVX: 83.91 (regime: mid) — oil volatility moderate‑to‑high; energy risk is a persistent factor. - Mortgage rate: 6.37% (condition: Neutral) — housing finance remains a headwind but stable. - Liquidity/positioning: mixed; signals favor selective, risk‑managed exposure rather than broad-risk levered bets. - Raw data is available in /data.
